page: Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core profile. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, and the Geekbench single-core score is in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth presents a notable tradeoff: while cached small-block read operations and large-block write operations perform at average levels, uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Large-scale LLM inference is also a limitation, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the bottom 10%.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average performance across database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, the D16as_v6 is suited for general-purpose workloads, web servers, and moderate database operations. Its hardware profile offers a standard resource density without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances, though users requiring high uncached memory throughput or local NVMe storage will need to consider alternative configurations.
description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 3.7 GHz, it features 8 physical cores and supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong text processing, while multi-core execution, database operations, and web serving throughput remain average. A key tradeoff is its weak uncached large-block read memory bandwidth, which falls in the bottom 25%. This virtual machine is suited for general-purpose computing, software compilation, and web serving workloads where balanced resource allocation is required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for balanced workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core execution, and a standard one-to-four vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage included, but supports Premium Storage options
Suited for general-purpose computing, web serving, and software compilation
